title: Juggling Video Annotation Schema v1.0
description: Ground-truth annotation for a single juggling video clip. Required fields must be filled by the primary annotator. contact_events is optional in v1.0.

annotation_schema_version.description: Schema version — must be '1.0' for this schema.

video_id.description: Stable identifier matching dataset_manifest.json. Format: jug_<difficulty>_<NNN> (e.g. jug_easy_001) or UUID from upload system.

filename.description: Original video filename (without path). Must match dataset_manifest.json.

source.description: Device or source used to record the video.

duration_seconds.description: Video duration in seconds (from ffprobe or manual measurement).

resolution.description: Width x Height of the processed video file (e.g. '1280x720').

fps.description: Detected frame rate of the video (from ffprobe).

difficulty.description: Subjective difficulty tier: easy (good conditions), medium (typical), hard (challenging).

total_juggling_count.description: Total number of juggling touches in the entire clip. Counted manually at 0.5x playback speed.

count_confidence.description: Annotator's confidence in the count. high = certain ±0, medium = ±1, low = ±2 or more.

dominant_body_part.description: The body part responsible for the majority of touches.

body_parts_used.description: Which body parts are used at least once in the clip.

ball_visible_quality.description: How clearly the ball is visible throughout the clip. excellent = always sharp; poor = frequently occluded/blurred.

lighting_quality.description: Overall lighting quality. variable = changes during clip (e.g. moving past windows).

camera_stability.description: How the camera was held. tracking = camera follows the player.

multi_person_present.description: True if more than one person is visible at any point in the clip.

multiple_balls_present.description: True if more than one ball is visible at any point in the clip.

expected_validity.description: Is this clip suitable for detection evaluation? valid = yes; borderline = usable but has issues; invalid = exclude from eval.

invalidity_reason.description: If expected_validity is not 'valid', describe why. Null otherwise.

notes.description: Free-text notes about anything unusual in this clip.

annotator.description: Initials or identifier of the primary annotator (e.g. 'ZL').

second_annotator.description: Initials of the second annotator if cross-check was done. Null if only one annotator.

second_annotator_count.description: total_juggling_count as determined by the second annotator. Null if no second annotator.

inter_annotator_agreement.description: True if both annotators agree within ±2. Null if no second annotator.

annotation_date.description: ISO 8601 date when annotation was completed (YYYY-MM-DD).

annotation_version.description: Annotation workflow version used. Must be 'v1.0' for this schema.

contact_events.description: Frame-level contact events. Optional in v1.0. Required for Phase D training data.

event_id.description: Sequential event number within this clip.

timestamp_ms.description: Approximate millisecond offset of the contact frame from video start.

side.description: Which side of the body made contact.

ball_height_approx_px.description: Approximate pixel Y-coordinate of the ball center at contact. Null if not estimated.

annotation_confidence.description: How confident the annotator is that this is a real contact event.

examples.notes: Clear light, static camera, ideal conditions.
